chapter: Chapter 5: The Fundamental Unit of Life

questions.question: Explain the concept of cell and its importance in living organisms.
questions.answer: A cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life. All living organisms are made up of cells. Cells perform essential functions such as metabolism, growth and reproduction.

questions.question: Explain the three statements of cell theory.
questions.answer: Cell theory states that all organisms are made of cells, the cell is the basic unit of life, and new cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Describe the structure and functions of the plasma membrane.
questions.answer: The plasma membrane surrounds the cell and regulates the movement of substances in and out. It is selectively permeable.

questions.question: Explain diffusion and its importance in cells.
questions.answer: Diffusion is movement of substances from higher concentration to lower concentration. It helps in exchange of gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Explain osmosis and its role in plants.
questions.answer: Osmosis is movement of water through a selectively permeable membrane. It helps plant roots absorb water.

questions.question: Explain the structure and functions of the cell wall.
questions.answer: The cell wall is a rigid layer made of cellulose present in plant cells. It provides protection and support.

questions.question: Explain the structure and functions of the nucleus.
questions.answer: The nucleus contains genetic material and controls cell activities. It is surrounded by a nuclear membrane.

questions.question: Explain the functions of cytoplasm in a cell.
questions.answer: Cytoplasm is the jelly-like substance containing organelles and is the site for most metabolic reactions.

questions.question: Describe the role of mitochondria in energy production.
questions.answer: Mitochondria generate energy in the form of ATP during cellular respiration.

questions.question: Explain the role of ribosomes in protein synthesis.
questions.answer: Ribosomes read genetic instructions and assemble amino acids to form proteins.

questions.question: Explain the functions of endoplasmic reticulum.
questions.answer: Endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes proteins and lipids and transports them within the cell.

questions.question: Explain the role of Golgi apparatus in the cell.
questions.answer: Golgi apparatus modifies, packages and transports materials within the cell.

questions.question: Explain the structure and functions of lysosomes.
questions.answer: Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes and help in breakdown of waste materials.

questions.question: Explain plastids and their types.
questions.answer: Plastids include chloroplasts, chromoplasts and leucoplasts which perform photosynthesis and storage functions.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of chloroplast.
questions.answer: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and help plants perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Explain the role of vacuoles in plant cells.
questions.answer: Vacuoles store water, nutrients and waste materials and maintain cell turgidity.

questions.question: Explain the structure of chromosomes.
questions.answer: Chromosomes are thread-like structures made of DNA and proteins that carry genetic information.

questions.question: Explain the role of DNA in heredity.
questions.answer: DNA stores genetic information and passes it from one generation to the next.

questions.question: Explain the concept of genes.
questions.answer: Genes are units of DNA responsible for inheritance of traits.

questions.question: Explain the process of mitosis.
questions.answer: Mitosis is cell division producing two identical daughter cells.

questions.question: Explain the process of meiosis.
questions.answer: Meiosis produces four daughter cells with half the chromosome number.

questions.question: Explain differences between plant and animal cells.
questions.answer: Plant cells have cell wall and chloroplasts whereas animal cells do not.

questions.question: Explain prokaryotic cells.
questions.answer: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

questions.question: Explain eukaryotic cells.
questions.answer: Eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

questions.question: Explain the importance of cell division.
questions.answer: Cell division is important for growth, repair of tissues and reproduction.
